pattern: Pool all character frequencies globally, greedily form pairs, then assign pairs to words sorted by length so shorter words get filled first (greedy by slot count).
hints: Since swaps are unrestricted across any positions in any words, only the total frequency of each character across all words matters, not which word each character is in.
Count total pairs available across all characters: each character with frequency f contributes floor(f/2) usable pairs, plus one leftover that can serve as a middle character for odd-length words.

Sort words by length; for each word greedily consume pairs to fill its floor(len/2) pair slots, then use a leftover odd character for odd-length words if available; count how many words reach full palindrome capacity.

edge_inputs.description: mismatched letters, only the single-char word is a palindrome
odd-length word needs one free odd character
only one odd character leftover, cannot fill two odd-length words centers
